Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD does not contain any `pkgver()` function, and the top-level scope consists solely of static variable assignments and array definitions. There are no command substitutions or embedded code executions at that level. The `source` array specifies a local file (`file://DaVinci_Resolve_${pkgver}_Linux.zip`) and a bundled script (`davinci-control-panels-setup.sh`), both of which are standard for AUR packages that require manual download of proprietary binaries. Checksums are provided for both sources. Since `prepare()`, `build()`, and `package()` are not executed during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`, there is no risk of unintended code execution at this stage. The file conforms to normal packaging practices.
</details>
<summary>No unsafe top-level or pkgver() code; source fetch is safe.</summary>
</security_assessment>

audits.body:
Cloning https://aur.archlinux.org/davinci-resolve.git to /home/mgalgs/aur-sleuth-data/bulk-reports/qwen-qwen3-235b-a22b-2507/aur-sleuth-baiqtdbr...

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a `source` array with a local file reference (`file://DaVinci_Resolve_${pkgver}_Linux.zip`) and a local script (`davinci-control-panels-setup.sh`), both of which are expected to be present in the build directory. No remote URLs or `git` sources are used, so no network downloads occur during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare`. The only code evaluated at parse time (outside of functions) consists of static variable assignments and array declarations — there is no command substitution, function calls, or execution of external commands in the global scope. The `pkgver()` function is not defined, so default behavior applies and no dynamic version fetching occurs. Therefore, sourcing this PKGBUILD for `makepkg --printsrcinfo` or downloading sources with `--nobuild` does not execute any potentially malicious code.

While the `prepare()` and `package()` functions contain significant logic (including extraction, `patchelf` usage, and udev rule installation), these stages are skipped under `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, so they are irrelevant for this narrow audit. The local source is expected and safe to fetch — it simply requires manual acquisition of a vendor-distributed blob, a common practice for proprietary software like DaVinci Resolve. No network access or code execution occurs during the targeted commands.

The `file://` source and absence of code in `pkgver()` or the top-level scope ensures that parsing this PKGBUILD is safe.
</details>
<summary>
No remote sources; no executors during parse
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No remote sources; no executors during parse[/]
